title: Theme Management
description: Safely inspect and manage WordPress themes. Operations: list, install, activate, update, delete. Registry-driven, approval-aware, health-verified. theme_delete is a CRITICAL destructive action: it requires confirm=true, confirmation_phrase="DELETE_THEME", and a reason; only inactive themes can be deleted.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The theme action to perform.

parameters.name: slug
parameters.description: The theme slug (required for all actions except theme_list).

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description: Must be true to execute theme_delete (destructive confirmation).

parameters.name: confirmation_phrase
parameters.description: Must equal "DELETE_THEME" to execute theme_delete.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Human-readable reason for the deletion; required for theme_delete.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Brief explanation of why this action is being requested. Shown to the human approver.

title: Plugin Management

Registry-driven, approval-aware, health-verified. plugin_delete is a CRITICAL destructive action: it requires confirm=true, confirmation_phrase="DELETE_PLUGIN", and a reason; only inactive plugins can be deleted and the plugin folder is backed up first.

title: Workflow Runtime
description: Create, list, get, update, delete, execute, import, export, and roll back multi-step operation workflows. A single workflow_execute approval covers every step (single approval); each step records an execution timeline (start/finish/duration) and its rollback_id (rollback awareness). The on_failure policy controls recovery — stop (default), continue, or rollback (auto-reverse completed steps). workflow_rollback reverses a past execution by execution_id. Supports executing any registered operation within steps.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Workflow action: workflow_list, workflow_get, workflow_create, workflow_update, workflow_delete, workflow_execute, workflow_import, workflow_export, workflow_history, workflow_rollback.

parameters.name: workflow_id
parameters.description: Workflow identifier (required for get, update, delete, execute, export).

parameters.name: steps
parameters.description: Ordered steps for create/update: each is { operation_id, payload }.

parameters.name: on_failure
parameters.description: workflow_execute failure policy: stop (default), continue, or rollback (auto-reverse completed steps).

parameters.name: execution_id
parameters.description: Execution identifier returned by workflow_execute (required for workflow_rollback).

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Brief explanation of why this action is being requested. Shown to the human approver.

title: File Access
description: Read files and browse the file tree under themes, plugins, and mu-plugins. Read-only. Paths are relative to wp-content, e.g. "themes/my-theme/functions.php" or "plugins/my-plugin/file.php" (a leading "wp-content/" or an absolute path is also accepted). Blocked paths (.env, wp-config.php, vendor/, keys, etc.) are denied and secrets are redacted. Actions: file_read, file_tree, file_metadata.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The file action to perform.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Path relative to wp-content, e.g. "themes/my-theme/functions.php" (a leading "wp-content/" or absolute path is also accepted). Required for file_read and file_metadata; optional for file_tree (omit to list the themes/plugins/mu-plugins roots).

title: Code Search
description: Search code under themes, plugins, and mu-plugins. Read-only. Blocked files are skipped and secrets in matches are redacted. Actions: search_text, search_symbol (function/class/hook), search_file (by name).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The search action to perform.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search term.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Limit the search to a directory relative to wp-content, e.g. "themes/my-theme" (a leading "wp-content/" or absolute path is also accepted). Omit to search all of themes/plugins/mu-plugins.

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of matches to return.

title: Patch Engine
description: Safely propose and apply file changes through the Patch Engine. Every apply snapshots the file first, verifies PHP syntax (php -l or tokenizer fallback), and auto-reverts on failure. Patches touching high-risk files (theme functions.php, active theme templates, plugin main files) require confirm=true and confirmation_phrase="APPLY_PATCH". Actions: patch_preview, patch_create, patch_apply, patch_verify, patch_status.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The patch action to perform.

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: Array of { path, modified } objects (required for patch_preview and patch_create). path is relative to wp-content, e.g. "themes/my-theme/functions.php" (a leading "wp-content/" or absolute path is also accepted); modified is the full new file content.

parameters.name: patch_id
parameters.description: Patch ID (required for patch_apply, patch_verify, patch_status).

parameters.name: explanation
parameters.description: Why the change is being made (patch_create).

parameters.name: risk_level
parameters.description: low | medium | high (patch_create).

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description: Required true to apply a patch touching a high-risk file.

parameters.name: confirmation_phrase
parameters.description: Must equal "APPLY_PATCH" to apply a patch touching a high-risk file.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Brief explanation of why this action is being requested. Shown to the human approver.

title: Rollback Engine

parameters.name: action

parameters.name: patch_id

title: Reporting Runtime
description: Generate read-only operational reports: report_site_health, report_plugin_health, report_security, report_content, report_woocommerce, report_agent_activity, report_approval_activity, report_patch_activity (and report_list to enumerate them). Inventory reports read WordPress/plugin data; activity reports aggregate the audit log. No writes, no rollback.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The report to generate.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Audit entries to scan for activity reports (default 1000, max 5000).

title: Media Enhancement Runtime
description: Read-only media-enhancement diagnostics. Foundation (100.3): media_enhance_capabilities (GD/Imagick + WebP/AVIF encode probe), image_sizes_list (registered sizes incl. theme add_image_size), image_size_usage_audit (per-size on-disk coverage), image_size_recommendations (unused/oversized sizes), image_size_verify (sizes present/missing/not_applicable for one attachment). Responsive audit (100.4): srcset_verify (WordPress srcset/sizes metadata for one attachment), responsive_image_audit (per-attachment responsive readiness with media_id, else a library-wide aggregate), missing_sizes_audit (attachments missing applicable sizes), image_size_context_audit (oversized/undersized original vs registered display sizes). Thumbnail regeneration (100.5, reversible writes): thumbnail_regenerate / thumbnail_regenerate_attachment (snapshot-backed; mode missing|all), thumbnail_regenerate_batch (cursor), thumbnail_verify (read). Regeneration captures a byte-for-byte snapshot before any write, verifies the result, and is reversible via the /media_enhance/rollback route. WebP (100.6, additive reversible writes): webp_audit / webp_verify (read coverage + capability), webp_generate / webp_generate_batch (create .webp sidecars beside originals — capability-gated, snapshot-backed, skips existing, never modifies/deletes originals; rollback deletes the generated .webp). Optimization (100.7, reversible re-encode): image_optimize_audit / image_optimize_verify (read coverage + estimate), image_optimize / image_optimize_batch (re-encode JPEG/PNG/WebP at a quality target to cut bytes without changing dimensions — capability-gated, snapshot-backed, skips insignificant savings; rollback restores original bytes via the /media_enhance/rollback route). Usage analysis (100.8, read-only cleanup intelligence): media_usage_scan (where one item is used across core content/blocks/WooCommerce/ACF/ACF-options/Elementor/theme_mods/options + classify active|indirect|unused|orphaned|cleanup_candidate), media_usage_report (library aggregate), unused_media_find, orphaned_media_find. Cleanup (100.9, guarded + reversible): unused_media_cleanup — re-verifies usage at execution time, hard-excludes WooCommerce/theme/draft/revision/unknown references, snapshots then TRASHES (never permanently deletes; DestructiveGuard CLEANUP_MEDIA required), reversible via /media_enhance/rollback.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The diagnostic, regeneration, WebP, optimization, usage-analysis, or cleanup action to run.

parameters.name: confirm
parameters.description: unused_media_cleanup: must be true (DestructiveGuard).

parameters.name: confirmation_phrase
parameters.description: unused_media_cleanup: must equal CLEANUP_MEDIA (DestructiveGuard).

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: unused_media_cleanup: human-readable reason for the cleanup (DestructiveGuard).

parameters.name: quality
parameters.description: image_optimize(_batch): re-encode quality 1-100 (default 82).

parameters.name: media_id
parameters.description: Attachment ID (required for image_size_verify, srcset_verify, image_size_context_audit, thumbnail_verify, thumbnail_regenerate; optional for responsive_image_audit — omit for a library-wide aggregate).

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description: thumbnail_regenerate(_batch): "missing" (default, only missing applicable sizes) or "all" (rebuild every registered size).

parameters.name: media_ids
parameters.description: thumbnail_regenerate_batch: explicit attachment IDs to process (otherwise the image library is scanned).

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: thumbnail_regenerate_batch: continuation cursor from a prior call.

parameters.name: rollback_id
parameters.description: Reverse a prior regeneration (via the /media_enhance/rollback route).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Scan/chunk bound: library audits default 500/max 5000; batch default 20/max 50.
